The OMOL25 official 4 million subset is a randomly sampled 4M-structure subset of the full Open Molecules 2025 (OMol25) dataset, which contains over 100 million DFT calculations at the ωB97M-V/def2-TZVPD level of theory spanning small molecules, biomolecules, metal complexes, and electrolytes. The full OMol25 dataset (100M structures) is already staged on S3; this 4M subset is the official structured split released by FAIR Chemistry and is currently missing from the project space on Shaheen III GPUP.

The subset serves as the primary supervised training corpus for the Adapt MLIP model — enabling training of molecular energy and force targets from scratch at a manageable scale before scaling to the full 100M dataset. It directly supports the project's objective of pretraining a machine learning interatomic potential (MLIP) on diverse small molecular data.

Aligns with the approved research topics Material Science (soft, composite, and nanomaterials) and Data Science and Engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Adapt is a KAUST-internal, unpublished variant of the UMA (Universal Models for Atoms) equivariant graph neural network architecture. The model has been significantly modified from the upstream UMA codebase to accommodate Shaheen III requirements — including MLflow integration for experiment tracking. It is an MLIP (machine learning interatomic potential) architecture that takes atomic position, atom type, charge, and spin as inputs and predicts energy and forces, aiming to replace traditional DFT calculations.

The model will be trained from scratch on the OMOL25 4M subset and other datasets on Shaheen III GPUP, directly implementing the project's core objective of developing a foundational MLIP through supervised pretraining. The developer is Gergo Ignacz, a researcher in the Ghanem group at KAUST.

Aligns with the approved research topics Material Science (soft, composite, and nanomaterials) and Data Science and Engineering.

The Adapt model is a KAUST-internal, unpublished modification of the UMA architecture. It is accessible at /tmp/IMS0000027 on the Ibex cluster.

Developer: Gergo Ignacz — researcher at KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology), Saudi Arabia.
KAUST is not a D5-country or D5-affiliated entity.

The upstream UMA base: https://huggingface.co/facebook/UMA (Meta FAIR, USA — Non-D5), previously reviewed and approved in the original AIMCR for this project.

Non-D5.

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
The Adapt model is an unpublished, in-house artifact developed by the Ghanem research group at KAUST. No external license applies.

The model is based on UMA, which is released under a permissive FAIR Chemistry License (see the UMA entry in this submission). KAUST's modifications are owned by KAUST.

The PI states Ownership/Licensing as open access (fairchem licence), indicating the intent to release under the FAIRChem/Meta licensing terms consistent with the upstream UMA model.

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes:
The model will be trained from scratch on the following datasets:
- OMOL25 4M subset (primary training corpus): 4 million DFT molecular structures
- Additional datasets to be used per the original project proposal (Open Catalyst, Open Materials, OpenDAC, In-house dataset)

This is not an LLM; training data volume is described as 100M datapoints (molecular structures), not tokens. D (token equivalent) is not applicable in the standard 6·N·D sense — see FLOPS Calculation.

The PI notes: '100M datapoints (this is not an LLM but a molecular dataset).'

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: Adapt will be trained from scratch on Shaheen III GPUP — it is not a fine-tuned version of a released model. The PI confirms: 'Full training from scratch.' No downstream fine-tuning tasks are documented for the released artifact, consistent with a score of 1 per rubric.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Adapt is based on the UMA architecture with approximately 30M trainable parameters.

N = 30 × 10^6 parameters

This is a molecular GNN, not an LLM. The PI provides measured throughput instead of a token count:
energy forward pass = 2.4 × 10^12 FLOP/step
full training step = 1.5 × 10^13 FLOP/step
per-GPU throughput = 1.5 × 10^13 × 1.04 steps/s ≈ 15 TFLOP/s
4-GPU aggregate ≈ 60 TFLOP/s ≈ 3.96 PFLOP/s (MFU ≈ 1.5%)

For the standard 6·N·D estimate, using N = 30 × 10^6 and the OMol25 4M subset as the primary dataset (4 × 10^6 structures; using the PI's statement of 100M datapoints total as D for the full training run):
6 × 30 × 10^6 × 100 × 10^6 = 1.8 × 10^16

1.8 × 10^16 is orders of magnitude below the 10^27 FLOPS threshold. 10^27 FLOPS check passed.

The PI also confirms (Q11): the project does not involve training or storing a proprietary model trained using more than 10^27 FLOPS.

No undocumented functionality or prohibited-use indicators were identified.
